# For Life First Aid

> PHECC certified Cardiac First Responder (CFR) training in Dundrum, Dublin, Ireland.

## About

For Life First Aid provides hands-on, PHECC-approved Cardiac First Responder courses taught by certified instructor Cathy Vard. Courses are held in Dundrum Town Centre, Dublin, with a maximum of six participants per class to ensure personalised training.

The CFR course covers CPR for adults, children, and infants; AED (defibrillator) use; choking management; recognition and response to cardiac arrest, heart attack, and stroke.

- Course fee: €85 (€50 deposit required at booking)
- Certification: PHECC Cardiac First Responder, valid for 2 years
- Duration: Approx. 3.5 hours
- Location: Unit CSA 2.5, Building 9, Sandyford Rd, Dundrum Town Centre, Dublin, D16 H7E2
- Instructor: Cathy Vard
- Phone: +353-86-896-1087
- Email: cathyvard@gmail.com

## Pages

- [Home](https://www.cardiacfirstrespondercourse.ie/): Course overview, details, testimonials, FAQ, and contact form
- [About](https://www.cardiacfirstrespondercourse.ie/about): About For Life First Aid and instructor Cathy Vard
- [Contact](https://www.cardiacfirstrespondercourse.ie/contact): Booking enquiries and contact form
- [Blog](https://www.cardiacfirstrespondercourse.ie/blog): First aid and CPR educational articles
- [Privacy Policy](https://www.cardiacfirstrespondercourse.ie/privacy): Privacy policy

## Blog Articles

- [What To Do If Someone Collapses](https://www.cardiacfirstrespondercourse.ie/blog/what-to-do-if-someone-collapses): Step-by-step guide for bystanders on what to do if someone collapses — checking for danger, starting CPR, and using an AED
- [How to Perform CPR Step by Step](https://www.cardiacfirstrespondercourse.ie/blog/how-to-perform-cpr): Complete guide to performing CPR on adults, children, and infants — technique, compression depth, rate, and when to use an AED
- [How to Use a Defibrillator](https://www.cardiacfirstrespondercourse.ie/blog/how-to-use-a-defibrillator): Step-by-step guide to using an AED — designed for complete beginners, no medical training required
- [Heart Attack vs Cardiac Arrest](https://www.cardiacfirstrespondercourse.ie/blog/heart-attack-vs-cardiac-arrest): Key differences between a heart attack and cardiac arrest, how to recognise each, and what to do
- [How Long Does a CPR Certificate Last?](https://www.cardiacfirstrespondercourse.ie/blog/how-long-does-cpr-certificate-last): How long a CPR or Cardiac First Responder certificate lasts in Ireland, when to renew, and what a refresher covers
- [How Much Does a CPR Course Cost in Dublin?](https://www.cardiacfirstrespondercourse.ie/blog/how-much-does-a-cpr-course-cost-in-dublin): What a CPR course costs in Dublin in 2025, what affects the price, and what is included in a PHECC-certified course
- [Best CPR Courses in Dublin](https://www.cardiacfirstrespondercourse.ie/blog/best-cpr-courses-in-dublin): What to look for before booking a CPR course in Dublin — certification, class size, practical training, and more
- [Is a Cardiac First Responder Course Worth It?](https://www.cardiacfirstrespondercourse.ie/blog/is-a-cardiac-first-responder-course-worth-it): Why a Cardiac First Responder course is worth doing — the confidence it builds and the lives it could help save
